runner_comment: Definite chance on his penultimate run; however, not the only front-runner in the field
runner_spotlight: Ran no race at Windsor in January; however, prior to that he went close in a Class 2 handicap at Sandown (2m, soft; effective on good to soft); on a handy mark if factoring in Harry Atkins' claim; front runner, and not the only one.
runner_quotes: 2023-11-04 - "It's great for Tristan and he gets on well with this horse. Knickerbockerglory has always promised to do well and he loves soft and heavy ground. We'll keep running in these handicaps over hurdles and hopefully he can have more success on this sort of ground - Tom Messenger, assistant trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "A lovely horse who seems to have been around for ages. He holds his form well, loves soft ground and bolted up first time last season at Sandown when he finally got his conditions. It looks like we'll have to be patient again this season but he'll be ready when the rain comes. 10-11-25"

runner_comment: Kept on to be under 5l behind Celtic Art over C&D last month; considered each-way
runner_spotlight: Hasn't had all that much racing since his last win 13 months ago and kept on to be under 5l behind Celtic Art over C&D last month; off the same mark so needs more, but one to consider each-way.
runner_stable_tour: "He's a full-brother to our very useful Malaya, who won five races over hurdles, notably Sandown's Imperial Cup. As he'd done plenty of pre-training in France before he came to us, we wanted to crack on with him and he looked the ideal sort for the new three-year-old National Hunt novice hurdles that have been introduced this season. He'd been showing us plenty at home, working well with Knappers Hill, but was disappointing at Newton Abbot and returned with a touch of sore shins. Obviously we won't be rushing him. 31-10-22"

runner_prev_owners: Ecurie Haras Des Beaux (own_1256508) - 2022-02-14, Etienne Raquin (own_1075896) - 2021-08-16
runner_stable_tour: "He'd made a successful jumping debut for us the season before last but failed to add to that last winter, when we found he was struggling with his breathing. That was sorted over the summer and he resumed in staying handicap hurdles at Wincanton last month where he hacked up by 13 lengths. Despite a 10lb rise, I think he can stay competitive and we'll look to find a similar contest in the next few weeks. 05-11-23"

race_race_name: Racing Welfare Supporting Mental Health Awareness Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
race_betting_forecast: 15/8 Inca De Lafayette, 2/1 Siam Park, 5/2 Move With The Beat, 13/2 Anyharminasking
runner_stable_tour: "He's always threatened to win a nice race but just hasn't quite done it yet. He ran very respectably on his comeback at Cheltenham and we might head to Haydock on Betfair Chase day for a 2m3f handicap hurdle next. There's a chance he'll go over fences at some point this season but, while the ground is good, there are races over hurdles for him between two miles and a two and a half. 06-11-24"

race_race_name: Racing Welfare Supporting Mental Health Awareness Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
race_betting_forecast: 15/8 Inca De Lafayette, 2/1 Siam Park, 5/2 Move With The Beat, 13/2 Anyharminasking

I think that's decent form and he improved from his first run, although he probably wants slightly better ground. 18-01-24"

race_race_name: Come Racing On 27th May Handicap Hurdle

runner_quotes: 2025-07-20 - "I think Stinginhisstep will be a nice horse, it might take another 12 months. He's not easy, but we've got him settled at home and he's better on the racecourse - Sue Gardner, trainer, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.", 2025-05-28 - "What impressed me was how well Springinhisstep stayed, he would be better over a further trip. He's progressive so we hope there's plenty more to come from him. We could tell this week he'd come on from his last race so we knew we had a good chance today. Sean [Houlihan, jockey] said he can't wait to get him over fences - Sue Gardner, trainer."

race_race_name: St. Austell Brewery Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
race_betting_forecast: 13/8 Hoe Joly Smoke, 15/8 Viroflay, 11/4 Certainly Red, 8/1 Lord Baddesley
runner_prev_trainers: Miss Olive Nicholls (trn_379359) - 2025-10-27, Olive Nicholls (trn_368379) - 2025-03-04, Georgina Nicholls (trn_131229) - 2023-11-25, Georgina Nicholls (trn_296361) - 2023-03-20, Paul Nicholls (trn_51903) - 2022-12-03, B De Montzey (trn_99621) - 2020-10-12
runner_prev_owners: Shearsmith, Bolton & Nicholls (own_1482736) - 2026-05-07, Paul Nicholls & John Bolton (own_1203556) - 2026-04-07, Paul Nicholls (own_123892) - 2023-03-24
runner_quotes: 2025-04-01 - "We've always thought very highly of Viroflay. He loved it out in front and he has plenty of speed. He was entered at Aintree this week but we thought we'd wait another year. He could go to the hunter chase meeting at Cheltenham [May 2] - Olive Nicholls, trainer, speaking to Racing TV."
runner_stable_tour: "Could be a surprise packet. Has developed into a very smart hunter chaser and my daughter Olive has now won seven times on him. His second to Its On The Line in the Champion Hunters' Chase at Punchestown was a top-class piece of form and it's just a shame he doesn't quite stay 3m; 2m6f is probably his best trip. Ran a super race at Ascot on Saturday and we can have a lot of fun with him this season. We had been thinking of the Rising Stars at Wincanton in five days' time but Olive wouldn't have been able to claim off him, so the novice handicap at Ascot two days ago looked more suitable. He shaped there as if 2m3f was on the sharp side so we'll go back up in trip next time. He's ideally suited by a flat, right-handed track. 04-11-25"

race_race_name: St. Austell Brewery Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
race_betting_forecast: 13/8 Hoe Joly Smoke, 15/8 Viroflay, 11/4 Certainly Red, 8/1 Lord Baddesley
runner_quotes: 2023-01-07 - "Certainly Red stays further than three miles so we knew he'd get home on soft ground. He's coming on nicely and will be on the go until about April - Lydia Richards, trainer.", 2022-12-01 - "We were fed up about Certainly Red's last run, he was brought down but it wasn't his fault at all. We've had to wait two and a half weeks for another race and he's been stood in his box wondering where he's going. I was pleased with that. Marc [Goldstein] said he was a little keen to start with, jumped well and then cantered away. He's been to Henrietta Knight's which has been the secret weapon. She did a little bit of work with him and his jumping today wasn't a problem at all - Lydia Richards, trainer.", 2022-03-19 - "The drying ground and the combination of that and going right-handed meant Certainly Red started hanging. He can jump better than that - Marc Goldstein, rider."

race_race_name: St. Austell Brewery Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
race_betting_forecast: 13/8 Hoe Joly Smoke, 15/8 Viroflay, 11/4 Certainly Red, 8/1 Lord Baddesley
runner_quotes: 2025-11-15 - "I said to Hoe Joly Smoke's owners I have to run this horse on the form he'd be showing us at home. He'd had a prep run and the obvious thing to do was drop back in drip - Dan Skelton, trainer.", 2025-03-08 - "Harry [Skelton] got off Hoe Joly Smoke immediately and said the Scottish National, but I just wouldn't want to go to Ayr and not run well, whereas we've got two weeks and we can come here to a track we know suits him. He's improving and everything about the bet365 will suit him. I like coming back to the scene of victories and obviously he likes this track. He's still a novice over hurdles, so next year we might have one run over hurdles, then find the nicest chase we can - Dan Skelton, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He ran really well on his reappearance in a 3m1f handicap chase at Cheltenham. He travelled into it like he was going to win and I can't say he blew up because we'd done a good bit with him at home, so I have to think he just didn't stay. We'll drop him back in trip next time and I think there could be a good prize heading his way. 10-11-25"

race_race_name: St. Austell Brewery Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
race_betting_forecast: 13/8 Hoe Joly Smoke, 15/8 Viroflay, 11/4 Certainly Red, 8/1 Lord Baddesley
runner_quotes: 2021-03-27 - "We ran Lord Baddesley at Cheltenham and he unseated and then his head went a bit, so it's great to get him back here - on a decent-galloping track and nice bit of ground. He still got quite hot coming here and is still quite a big, buzzy creature, but during the race he did it well, which is the main thing. He won't have a hard time and we might call it a day for the season, although we could have a look at another one. He'll be a lovely chaser next season - Chris Gordon, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "We didn't learn much about him in the Badger Beer. We're not sure whether to aim low or high yet - there's a few ideas, but he's probably due a win and I can see him landing a nice pot this season. 14-11-24", "We are now buying nicer young horses and this fellow is typical of that type of individual. Following two encouraging bumper efforts last season he made a winning debut over hurdles this season at Fontwell. He raced very clean and deliberate, which maybe cost him the odd length early on but as he warmed up he became very slick. There is an EBF qualifier at Wincanton coming up that I would like to run him in as it is over 2m which is his trip. If we can get him in the first four then it gives us another string to our bow at the end of the season. I will be minding this fellow this season as it will be when he goes chasing next year he will really start to show his strengths. 11-11-20"

runner_stable_tour: "He ran really well when winning at Taunton last week to qualify and he should have a good chance. in the Hunter Chase at Cheltenham. We'll have two in the race but he looks our number one. 15-02-26"

runner_stable_tour: "His comeback second at Warwick the other day was a really good run. It was his first chase and I was a bit concerned beforehand because it looked very competitive and there were a lot of runners but he ran with enormous credit. The front two pulled clear but he's only gone up 4lb for that so should still be competitive. 23-11-23", "He's a tough little horse who always wears his heart on his sleeve. He galloped all the way to the line when second on his reappearance at Exeter last week. He'll be out again in two or three weeks and hopefully he'll be able to continue to give connections plenty of fun over the winter. 30-10-24"

race_betting_forecast: 1/2 Golden Son, 5/2 Regatta De Blanc, 12/1 High Game Royal, 20/1 Est Illic, 25/1 Get Off Your Phone, 33/1 Fongs Way
runner_quotes: 2024-01-08 - "We were going to run Regatta De Blanc in another point but that was waterlogged off so we decided to come here instead - Will Biddick, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "She was six-from-seven for Will Biddick in point-to-points and hunter chases, and shaped as if mares' hurdles are well within her compass before getting a bit tired on her first run for us at Chepstow last month in a novice event. She's sure to come on a lot for that and looks attractively handicapped on 116, but we'll go up in trip and back over fences now. She's got multiple options coming up and I'm looking forward to seeing what she's capable of as she's a decent mare. 04-11-24"

runner_quotes: 2026-03-08 - "After the last day we thought we might try Newton Rambler over this trip and make good use of him and we got it spot on. I should've sat quiet on him at the second-last and asked him for a long stride when I didn't need, but the horse knows what he's doing - Oakley Brown, jockey, speaking to Racing TV."

race_race_name: Murrayshall Country Estate Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
race_betting_forecast: 5/4 Next Twist, 5/1 Newtown Rambler,  Shadowfax Of Rohan, 13/2 Hypotenus, 12/1 Mister Cessna, 14/1 Garde Des Champs,  Dance Thief
runner_prev_trainers: Joe Tickle (trn_263574) - 2026-04-15, Gavin Cromwell (trn_160524) - 2024-12-21, John C McConnell (trn_132759) - 2023-09-25, C Laffon-Parias (trn_64332) - 2022-11-21
runner_quotes: 2026-01-08 - "I was really pleased, as Hypotenus's last run was a horrible experience for him. He came back from that battered and bruised, with cuts and marks all over him, which was frustrating. He's not the type to endure a hustle or bustle; he likes to get into his own rhythm. I was wondering whether his confidence would be okay after Ludlow. He did have to lug a big weight, but I was still hoping for a big race. He ran some nice races for us last year without winning; he was beaten by Vincenzo and everything that beat him looked like a nice horse - Joe Tickle, trainer.", 2023-09-29 - "We were hoping coming here that there would be a bit of pace for Hypotenus so that we could drop in a small bit but nobody wanted to make it so Keith kept it simple. He went a good gallop on testing ground - Gavin Cromwell, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He's another newcomer to the yard and made a winning start in a maiden hurdle at Gowran at the end of September. The ground was heavy there, but he's a better horse on better ground - he has plenty of gears and travels a bit too strong in that winter ground, so two miles is far enough for him in it. He's definitely better going right-handed but we're going to give him a go at Naas for a four-year-old winners' race on Sunday. 10-11-23"

runner_quotes: 2025-10-30 - "It went to plan, and it was lovely to win. Sir Carnegie's jumping keeps improving and he stays very well. If he'd jumped as well at Sedgefield last time, he'd have won. We ran him in a chase at Hexham, but he was jumping a bit novicey, which is why we went back to hurdles. He's had a few falls over hurdles, and you can see he's just getting his confidence up now as he's getting better with every race. He stays all day, and we'll just keep getting him relaxed. He's growing with every run, and hopefully he'll just keep improving - Mickey Bowen, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He's bouncing around the place and will be ready in about two weeks. There's a nice 0-100 novice handicap chase at Newcastle and I know it's a long way to travel there from our base in Wales, but we think he can win it. He'll take a lot of beating in it. 26-03-26"

runner_quotes: 2025-10-17 - "Kinswoman is all speed. She ran well last week when just getting touched off and she's getting the hang of it now. She'll get better as she gets stronger and hopefully she'll be one for next year - Maureen Haggas, trainer's wife.", 2025-08-11 - "I think we've found the key to Kinswoman. She's not just fast mentally, she is actually fast. She relaxed well, took a breath - which on a couple occasions she hasn't done - and it's the difference between going and seeing out that last furlong and stepping into the race well and not. She did what you'd hope she'd do but you were taking on trust that coming back to five [furlongs] was going to work - Tom Marquand, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing."

race_betting_forecast: 2/1 Kinswoman, 5/1 Almaty Star, 6/1 Grandlad, 9/1 Nogo's Dream, 10/1 Michaela's Boy,  Mesaafi, 11/1 Rocking Ends, 14/1 Forager, 18/1 Naana's Sparkle,  Twilight Fun, 20/1 Existent, 25/1 Alondra, 33/1 Cressida Wildes

runner_prev_trainers: Adrian McGuinness (trn_126792) - 2025-12-09, Robert Cowell (trn_108513) - 2025-10-27, Michael Appleby (trn_93267) - 2024-05-16, Richard Hughes (trn_280692) - 2022-08-26
runner_quotes: 2026-01-12 - "Michaela's Boy was rated much higher last winter and he's got himself nicely handicapped today, off the mark of 80 I knew he was very capable, but it was a question of what sort of form he was in. Robert's got him in great form and I was able to get that stalking position behind Almaty Star. I knew he was the horse to beat, so it worked out lovely. He's only won by three-quarters of a length, so you'd like to think the handicapper can't get too carried away. - Luke Morris, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing", 2022-04-04 - "Shane rode Michaela's Boy two weeks ago and said he might need it, but he was professional and I knew he wasn't going to run a bad race. He's a straightforward horse. He went to post really good and has been cantering on that ground at home. We might go to Chester for the Lily Agnes - Richard Hughes, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He's a lovely chestnut we bought from Richard Hughes's yard and he has done nothing but please us. He's won his last two races for us at Newcastle and Chelmsford and, having arrived with a mark of 78, will be starting this season on 94. Although that's 16lb higher I think he's definitely capable of improving again. He was quite quirky when he came but we've ironed out his problems and I think we can go even further with him now as he's a much stronger individual. He finished down the field in a 5½f race at Chantilly on Tuesday, but hopefully he'll improve on that and he may go to all-weather finals day at Newcastle. The only problem is that the sprint there is over 6f and he didn't get that trip last year. That's not to say he won't now that he has bulked up, but I would prefer it if the race were over 5f. 22-03-23"

runner_quotes: 2025-06-23 - "There's a tailwind there so speed is more likely to carry on but Nogo's Dream is in great shape - Oisin Murphy, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing."
runner_stable_tour: "Some people don't like him but I love him. If every horse in the yard was second nearly every time they ran you'd be happy. He's a bit quirky and we took all the gear off him for Sandown on Saturday, when he finished fourth after getting no luck in running. But that's Nogo, you have to ride him for luck and we didn't get it. He's ultra-consistent and it's just about finding the key to him. 18-09-24"

runner_quotes: 2026-01-04 - "I'm absolutely delighted. To see Existent get his head in front was a thrill and hopefully he's turned over a new leaf. Jack gave him a good ride because you can't bully him at all and try to put pressure on him early on to pick up. He was third in the Dash last year, and we'll have to go up in the weights a bit, but that will be the long-term plan - Stuart Williams, trainer, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.", 2025-06-07 - "Rose [Dawes] has given Existent a good ride and steered him through pretty well. No complaints there, and we are very pleased with the run. He's won nearly 50,000GBP in prize money this year just from being second and third - Stuart Williams, trainer.", 2022-04-30 - "Existent is a nice straightforward horse to deal with and hopefully he will now go on to the King's Stand Stakes. Tom (Morley, owner) and his mum have won the race twice before and we entered him for it last week. It is a bit of an ask, being rated 101, but we think he definitely warranted an entry and I think it should suit him. I think five is his best trip for us - Stuart Williams, trainer.", 2020-10-03 - "The race didn't go right at Ascot and Existent didn't come out of the race the way we would have liked. He's a strong horse now and as long as he's in good order then we'll look to go to the Horris Hill with him - John Gosden, trainer."

race_race_name: Sky Bet Race To The Ebor Jorvik Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_betting_forecast: 3/1 Klassleader, 5/1 Sing Us A Song, 11/2 Plage De Havre, 6/1 Stressfree, 7/1 Will Scarlet, 8/1 Paddy The Squire, 12/1 Fireblade, 14/1 Prince Of The Seas,  Dark Moon Rising, 33/1 King's Code,  Saint Etienne
runner_quotes: 2025-07-05 - "Plage De Havre is a horse we think a lot of and we never had an anxious moment. He needs a strong gallop and a bit of cover to settle and he got everything today. He is as good as he looks. The Ebor is a possibility although I'll have to discuss it with his owner as he is convinced a  mile and a half is his trip, but we're going to have limited options there so it might be worth another try at going a bit further. He's definitely settling better now - Andrew Balding, trainer."

race_race_name: Sky Bet Race To The Ebor Jorvik Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_betting_forecast: 3/1 Klassleader, 5/1 Sing Us A Song, 11/2 Plage De Havre, 6/1 Stressfree, 7/1 Will Scarlet, 8/1 Paddy The Squire, 12/1 Fireblade, 14/1 Prince Of The Seas,  Dark Moon Rising, 33/1 King's Code,  Saint Etienne
runner_quotes: 2025-05-08 - "Paddy The Squire had been off a long time and he'd been gelded but he's improved for gelding and the better ground today helped him. He has some nice form and I hope he can progress. This track seems to suit him - Iain Jardine, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He's taken a massive leap forward - he's gone up 20lb this season. Gelding him at the end of last season has been a massive bonus. It has helped him concentrate and relax. He won first time out at Chester over 1m2f and we've gradually moved him up in trip, which is bringing out improvement. He won the Lanark Silver Bell with his ears pricked and then ran well in the Old Borough Cup. He's a lazy horse at home but he keeps it for the track. He'll have another run this season but he won't be overdone. He could improve again next season and the Ebor will be his main target. If he was to run a massive race in that, races like the Melbourne Cup could come into play. 10-09-25"
runner_medical: 2024-02-22 - Wind Surgery, 2023-07-19 - Wind Surgery, 2023-04-04 - Wind Surgery

race_race_name: Sky Bet Race To The Ebor Jorvik Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_betting_forecast: 3/1 Klassleader, 5/1 Sing Us A Song, 11/2 Plage De Havre, 6/1 Stressfree, 7/1 Will Scarlet, 8/1 Paddy The Squire, 12/1 Fireblade, 14/1 Prince Of The Seas,  Dark Moon Rising, 33/1 King's Code,  Saint Etienne
runner_quotes: 2025-05-31 - "Stressfree has been very consistent, he's got a lot of ability but he's often the bridesmaid. We sent him hurdling through the winter to try to toughen him up more than anything. It was an excellent ride by Danny but I'm never confident with this horse because he has come out on the wrong end of a battle before. He did nothing wrong today and it was a great performance. He could go to Ascot for the mile-and-half handicap and I think he'd get the trip in the Ebor. He keeps a little bit back so you wouldn't know how good he is - David O'Meara, trainer.", 2025-02-16 - "Stressfree is a very talented horse on the Flat. It's only his second go today and Brian (Hughes) was pulling it up in front, and I knew we were going to quicken up the straight, so I was just hoping that his jumping was going to hold up, which it has done. There'll be a bigger picture for this horse I'm sure - Henry Brooke, jockey, speaking to Racing TV."
runner_stable_tour: "He should have won at Doncaster last time - he was all dressed up with nowhere to go. I ran him over hurdles over the winter to toughen him up a bit as he's a horse with a lot of ability who can be a bit soft. He could go for the City and Suburban next and might be one for the John Smith's Cup. 14-04-25"

race_race_name: Sky Bet Race To The Ebor Jorvik Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_betting_forecast: 3/1 Klassleader, 5/1 Sing Us A Song, 11/2 Plage De Havre, 6/1 Stressfree, 7/1 Will Scarlet, 8/1 Paddy The Squire, 12/1 Fireblade, 14/1 Prince Of The Seas,  Dark Moon Rising, 33/1 King's Code,  Saint Etienne
runner_quotes: 2025-11-03 - "King's Cove was qualified and those finals are good races to have because I wouldn't win many £80,000 handicaps. There was prize-money down to sixth, so I thought he'd get some of it. He's had a fair few runs and he's had a bit of a break, but he came back and won at the track last month, and his last three races have been good. He likes the all-weather - he prefers that to the grass - so we might get him back out in two or three weeks - David Evans, trainer.King's Cove was tough. He jumped good and I didn't really want to lead but I felt like I had the race in control from where I was. It took a while to build him up but once he got his head down and the more I asked him, the more grit he showed me - Neil Callan, jockey, speaking on Racing TV.", 2023-06-09 - "King's Code is a big lad and, with the ground as quick as it was, I thought he might struggle a bit, which he did early doors. The cheekpieces helped him concentrate, the penny dropped late and he closed really well - Sean Levey, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing."

race_race_name: Sky Bet Race To The Ebor Jorvik Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_betting_forecast: 3/1 Klassleader, 5/1 Sing Us A Song, 11/2 Plage De Havre, 6/1 Stressfree, 7/1 Will Scarlet, 8/1 Paddy The Squire, 12/1 Fireblade, 14/1 Prince Of The Seas,  Dark Moon Rising, 33/1 King's Code,  Saint Etienne
runner_quotes: 2026-03-13 - "Ed [Crisford] was quite confident if we could get in front, as all Will Scarlet does is gallop, and he was right - Ray Dawson, jockey, speaking on Racing Dubai YouTube channel."

race_race_name: Sky Bet Race To The Ebor Jorvik Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_betting_forecast: 3/1 Klassleader, 5/1 Sing Us A Song, 11/2 Plage De Havre, 6/1 Stressfree, 7/1 Will Scarlet, 8/1 Paddy The Squire, 12/1 Fireblade, 14/1 Prince Of The Seas,  Dark Moon Rising, 33/1 King's Code,  Saint Etienne
runner_quotes: 2024-06-02 - "Dark Moon Rising is a talented horse when he's on a good day. He was very well handicapped on his old form. I don't know what was up with him, he hasn't been performing. But he travelled really strongly and galloped all the way to the line. He won cosily - Shane Gray, rider, talking to ITV.", 2021-06-22 - "Dark Moon Rising is a work in progress, he was very green first time. I thought he'd be a bit wiser today but he was green again. But once he passed one he picked the bridle up and he travelled well into the race. He came good from two down, he knuckled down and galloped through the line well. He'll keep improving with racing. He's a big, laid back horse at home, he's just going to have to learn with racing - Kevin Ryan, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He had some high-class form at three and if he can revert to the form he showed in the Dante he's on a very fair mark and could be a nice horse for handicaps. I think ten furlongs is his best trip and he'll benefit from getting back on a conventional track after not handling Pontefract last time. We retain a lot of faith in him. 03-05-23"

runner_quotes: 2024-03-03 - "That went to plan really. Very smooth, very simple A to B. When I gave him a squeeze Pocklington put the race to bed very well. I thought Hollie (Doyle, jockey on runner-up) would make it but I didn't want it to turn into too much of a sprint because he hit a bit of a flat spot the last day. I thought the quicker we get through the gears and striding out, the more chance I've got. He's an exciting horse - Harry Russell, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing."

race_betting_forecast: 6/1 Binhareer, 9/1 Fahrenheit Seven,  Pocklington,  Sondad,  Tuco Salamanca, 11/1 Pilgrim, 12/1 Tropical Storm,  Dark Thirty, 14/1 Strike Red,  Fast Track Harry,  Desert Falcon,  Kylian,  Rousing Encore, 16/1 American Style,  Russet Gold, 25/1 Woven,  Dark Cloud Rising, 33/1 Indian Run,  Fandom, 40/1 Wicket Keeper,  We Never Stop, 50/1 Sir Yoshi
runner_owner: Middleham Park Racing Xii & Mrs G Cowell
runner_quotes: 2023-07-07 - "Kylian has been quite a slow learner. Karl always thought he was a good horse, and he was surprised when he got beat at York first time out, but he is probably just a slow learner and he is learning through his racing. Hopefully he will go on to better things. Ryan thinks he could possibly get six furlongs and that is another avenue. The Molecomb could possibly be next - Philip Robinson, assistant racing manager to the owner."
runner_stable_tour: "This colt has been showing up well at home and is ready to go now. He has been doing everything we have asked and looks nice, and he is ready to run. 07-05-23"

race_betting_forecast: 6/1 Binhareer, 9/1 Fahrenheit Seven,  Pocklington,  Sondad,  Tuco Salamanca, 11/1 Pilgrim, 12/1 Tropical Storm,  Dark Thirty, 14/1 Strike Red,  Fast Track Harry,  Desert Falcon,  Kylian,  Rousing Encore, 16/1 American Style,  Russet Gold, 25/1 Woven,  Dark Cloud Rising, 33/1 Indian Run,  Fandom, 40/1 Wicket Keeper,  We Never Stop, 50/1 Sir Yoshi
runner_quotes: 2024-05-29 - "Sir Yoshi wants quicker ground than that. He got a bit intimidated last day and I wanted to get one more run into him before Ascot. He's tough and we don't really know how good he is. He'll go for the Windsor Castle now - David Marnane, trainer"

race_betting_forecast: 6/1 Binhareer, 9/1 Fahrenheit Seven,  Pocklington,  Sondad,  Tuco Salamanca, 11/1 Pilgrim, 12/1 Tropical Storm,  Dark Thirty, 14/1 Strike Red,  Fast Track Harry,  Desert Falcon,  Kylian,  Rousing Encore, 16/1 American Style,  Russet Gold, 25/1 Woven,  Dark Cloud Rising, 33/1 Indian Run,  Fandom, 40/1 Wicket Keeper,  We Never Stop, 50/1 Sir Yoshi

runner_quotes: 2025-10-11 - "Binhareer is just really coming to himself now. He's learned to relax and we've figured out his trip. He appreciated the dig in the ground today - I know it says good but it's easier and he loved that. He felt super comfortable. The tempo was hot, which suited him as well. It was probably the perfect recipe to make him look good but he did that in good fashion - Tom Marquand, jockey.", 2025-07-07 - "I think the step back in trip has definitely helped Binhareer. He has been keen but he relaxed really well today and, when I got to the two [furlong pole] I felt I was always going to pick them up. I'm really happy with him and he won nicely - Oisin Orr, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.", 2024-10-05 - "Binhareer still has plenty to learn but he'll be a nice horse - Rossa Ryan, jockey."

race_betting_forecast: 6/1 Binhareer, 9/1 Fahrenheit Seven,  Pocklington,  Sondad,  Tuco Salamanca, 11/1 Pilgrim, 12/1 Tropical Storm,  Dark Thirty, 14/1 Strike Red,  Fast Track Harry,  Desert Falcon,  Kylian,  Rousing Encore, 16/1 American Style,  Russet Gold, 25/1 Woven,  Dark Cloud Rising, 33/1 Indian Run,  Fandom, 40/1 Wicket Keeper,  We Never Stop, 50/1 Sir Yoshi
runner_quotes: 2026-02-27 - "Fast Track Harry looks to have improved and matured. That was a solid step forward on the all-weather for the first time. It was a very pleasing comeback. We'd need to run a few more times to qualify for the All-Weather Championships and I'm not sure we'd look to do that. It might be a bit tight but we've won a good prize here - Clive Cox, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He showed promise last year when he made a winning debut at Newbury, then found the ground a little too soft when we ran him in a Listed race at Doncaster. He's a big, strong horse and is coming to the boil nicely now. We're looking forward to seeing him again. 17-04-25"

race_betting_forecast: 6/1 Binhareer, 9/1 Fahrenheit Seven,  Pocklington,  Sondad,  Tuco Salamanca, 11/1 Pilgrim, 12/1 Tropical Storm,  Dark Thirty, 14/1 Strike Red,  Fast Track Harry,  Desert Falcon,  Kylian,  Rousing Encore, 16/1 American Style,  Russet Gold, 25/1 Woven,  Dark Cloud Rising, 33/1 Indian Run,  Fandom, 40/1 Wicket Keeper,  We Never Stop, 50/1 Sir Yoshi
runner_quotes: 2025-09-20 - "I thought we might just get our nose in front - it was a head-bob. I'm delighted with Desert Falcon as he's run a cracker. That trip is all right for him on that type of ground and he's shown that he's versatile and can compete in these big sprint handicaps - Rowan Scott, jockey."
runner_stable_tour: "He's won three of his last four and I wouldn't say I've been surprised by him. He's been in great form. He didn't look as if he'd stopped improving last time when he won at Haydock and he got a really nice positive ride from Ryan Sexton, who grabbed the race by the scruff of the neck and was always in control. He loves a turning 7f, but he's the sort you might put in the Ayr Gold Cup and think about the Silver Cup with him because they'll go quick - he's not a slow horse at all and you can see him coming home strongly. The only thing about him is that he wouldn't want the ground too soft. He's enjoying the dry summer when one or two others aren't. 23-07-25"
runner_medical: 2026-03-20 - Wind Surgery, 2024-05-23 - Wind Surgery, 2022-11-15 - Wind Surgery

runner_quotes: 2026-04-04 - "I thought the ground would beat Pilgrim but he's a bit older. We've had a long spell off with him, he had a couple of runs on the all-weather which helped him get back. He is a turf horse and he's capable off his mark and showed it today - David Barron, joint-trainer.", 2024-06-21 - "Pilgrim has done it well there. I thought he might have knackered his handicap mark at Musselburgh. We always thought that he was a really nice horse, not just a good two-year-old, and he's shown there today he's good. It's a great place to come and have a winner so it's very nice. This lad has a lot of speed. He travelled very well and he won quite comfortably. He done it easy - Joe Fanning, rider."

race_betting_forecast: 6/1 Binhareer, 9/1 Fahrenheit Seven,  Pocklington,  Sondad,  Tuco Salamanca, 11/1 Pilgrim, 12/1 Tropical Storm,  Dark Thirty, 14/1 Strike Red,  Fast Track Harry,  Desert Falcon,  Kylian,  Rousing Encore, 16/1 American Style,  Russet Gold, 25/1 Woven,  Dark Cloud Rising, 33/1 Indian Run,  Fandom, 40/1 Wicket Keeper,  We Never Stop, 50/1 Sir Yoshi

runner_prev_owners: Stonestreet Stables LLC & Biagi Thoroughbreds (own_901308) - 2026-02-12, Stonestreet Stables LLC (own_579092) - 2025-08-27
runner_stable_tour: "He won his only race on turf at Keeneland by almost seven lengths and we're going to target either the Coventry Stakes or the Windsor Castle. I haven't had much luck in the Coventry. It's a race everyone and their brother seems to want to win and it's produced many good horses over the years. He's a big colt and, physically, you'd think he'd be able to run the three-quarters of a mile in the Coventry, but it will be determined when we get a little closer and we see how both races are shaping up. He's by Showcasing out of a Pivotal mare and definitely has an affinity with the turf. We put him and American Rascal together in a workout and I'd have to say American Rascal was better, but Fandom is very straightforward, came out blazing at Keeneland and has trained very well since. 12-06-23"

race_betting_forecast: 4/1 Time For Sandals,  Big Mojo, 5/1 Kind Of Blue, 13/2 American Affair, 8/1 Cool Hoof Luke, 10/1 Aramram, 12/1 Quinault,  Regional, 14/1 Elmonjed, 16/1 Jasour, 20/1 Diligent Harry, 25/1 Crestofdistinction, 33/1 Ain't Nobody
runner_quotes: 2025-09-06 - "We were disappointed with Big Mojo's last run at Goodwood and there were a lot of question marks by his name, but we put it down to the ground. He's gone and proved he's still at the top. Everything went to plan, it took William half the track to pull him up. It's great for the owners - Mick Appleby, trainer.We knew we just needed another July Cup performance when we were only inches away from winning. We knew Big Mojo was capable, we've just hit the bar a couple times and been unlucky. He came fourth at the Breeders' Cup last year when he handled Del Mar really well and we wouldn't be scared to go back. We think he's equally good over five or six and we've got a few options as he's got an entry on Champions Day and we might even consider the Abbaye - Paul Teasdale, owner.It was a brilliant spare ride. I was actually supposed to ride Big Mojo in the Sandy Lane earlier this season but we [Godolphin] ran Symbol Of Honour at the last minute. It has all come together and we've got the job done today. It was genuinely beautiful good ground today. I thought middle to stand side is probably where you wanted to be. The way the race and our draw [15] panned out was very helpful but he still put up a very good performance today. He was very smooth, and I didn't really have a moment of worry at all ? he just got on with it and saw it out well - William Buick, jockey.", 2025-07-12 - "It was a great run, although I'd have been happier if we'd won! Big Mojo has run a blinder and it's good to see him bounce back to form. We'll probably drop back to five furlongs now as his owner (Paul Teasdale) wants to win the Nunthorpe as he comes from Yorkshire - Mick Appleby, trainer.", 2025-04-30 - "I'd think the King Charles would be more likely. Training on is always a concern if you've got a decent two-year-old, but at home Big Mojo had been showing us he had. He's been working really well against some of the older horses - Mick Appleby, trainer.", 2024-07-31 - "It's great, Big Mojo is an exciting horse and he's improved a lot since his debut. It's a nice way to break a maiden, in a Group 3 race, and he's a pro horse. He's only going to improve. He's got a lot of speed and I like him over six as well. He's a quality horse going forward, he's strong physically but mentally he's not there yet - Silvestre de Sousa, rider."

race_betting_forecast: 4/1 Time For Sandals,  Big Mojo, 5/1 Kind Of Blue, 13/2 American Affair, 8/1 Cool Hoof Luke, 10/1 Aramram, 12/1 Quinault,  Regional, 14/1 Elmonjed, 16/1 Jasour, 20/1 Diligent Harry, 25/1 Crestofdistinction, 33/1 Ain't Nobody
runner_quotes: 2025-08-22 - "I'm not one to bring horses to make up the numbers. I never have done it and never will. This is not about being proved right, it's having faith in the horse. I told the owners Ain't Nobody was in great form and I said we were going to ride him cold, they're going to go hard here. We'd ride him like when he won at Royal Ascot. It's worked out a treat - Kevin Ryan, trainer.", 2024-06-19 - "It panned out perfectly. Obviously it was such a big field you don't know what's going to happen. After going 100 yards, I was following the American horse, getting a good lead and he was always going to bring me as far as I wanted, and Ain't Nobody was tough at the finish. He's a nice horse for the future, he's only a young horse on his second run, so plenty to look forward to - Jamie Spencer, rider."

race_betting_forecast: 4/1 Time For Sandals,  Big Mojo, 5/1 Kind Of Blue, 13/2 American Affair, 8/1 Cool Hoof Luke, 10/1 Aramram, 12/1 Quinault,  Regional, 14/1 Elmonjed, 16/1 Jasour, 20/1 Diligent Harry, 25/1 Crestofdistinction, 33/1 Ain't Nobody
runner_quotes: 2025-06-17 - "I've trained the family for three generations so it's very sweet. American Affair's grandfather had his day here so we're pretty lucky that we can breed horses like that. He's just getting faster, we went seven furlongs at Wetherby in his maiden, we've learnt to run him over the right trip, we took him back to five and he's just getting better and better. I was quite confident he could do it today, he had the talent, we got it wrong at Haydock. I knew he was probably one of the fastest horses in the race but it's how you control that. We've come down a day early and got him relaxed and it all fell into place - Jim Goldie, trainer.I first won his maiden on him over seven furlongs round Wetherby, it's hard to believe. And then American Affair started getting sharper and sharper and I rode him one day at York over seven and he proper latched on and I said to Jim 'I think this lad could be a sprinter' and obviously he's just got quicker as he's got older as do a lot of sprinters - Paul Mulrennan, jockey.", 2025-05-15 - "It's probably American Affair's last go in a handicap. He's done nothing but improve and started off last year in the mid-70s, but the more racing he's had the sharper he's got. He just shuts down when he hits the front and is still quite raw. Jim said we'd learn a lot more about him today as there's no easy five-furlong race here. He's proved he's up to going to the next level - Paul Mulrennan, rider.", 2025-04-19 - "We probably have to go into Group races. There's the King Charles over five furlongs at Ascot, which would be okay. I don't think we'll go to Newmarket but American Affair is a good horse and he's maturing - Jim Goldie, trainer.", 2024-09-14 - "American Affair has done nothing but improve all year. Jim Goldie is some trainer. The last couple of races he's been running with the choke out and not really finishing. Jim said today to ride him ice cold, I got him to switch off and he finished really well. What will he be like next year with another winter on his back? He's still very babyish and raw - Paul Mulrennan, jockey.", 2024-04-21 - "American Affair is a homebred and as he came off the box, I said to him, 'Your granny [Class Wan] won here' and we've had a few generations of this breed. From where he was drawn [13], Paul got the rail and kind of bossed it - Jim Goldie, trainer, speaking to ITV Racing.", 2023-05-16 - "American Affair is a work in progress but the uphill finish and the step up to seven played to his strengths. He's a big horse and there should be a lot more to come from him - Paul Mulrennan, jockey, speaking on Racing TV."
runner_stable_tour: "He's very talented. We hoped he would bounce back the way he did at Musselburgh and he'll improve for it. We're excited. He'll probably go to York for a 5f handicap next but I've put him in the Temple Stakes and the King Charles at Royal Ascot. We'll probably stick to 5f at the moment. I think he's up to that class over five and he's a simpler ride over that trip. He appears very fast and seems to be comfortable at that pace. 07-05-25"

race_betting_forecast: 4/1 Time For Sandals,  Big Mojo, 5/1 Kind Of Blue, 13/2 American Affair, 8/1 Cool Hoof Luke, 10/1 Aramram, 12/1 Quinault,  Regional, 14/1 Elmonjed, 16/1 Jasour, 20/1 Diligent Harry, 25/1 Crestofdistinction, 33/1 Ain't Nobody

He ended up being in front a big long but he was brave once he realised the horse on his far side started to give him a race, and knuckled back down. He's a lovely, genuine horse and he's been super competitive in some hard races - Tom Marquand, jockey."
runner_stable_tour: "He showed he's still improving when winning a Listed race at Doncaster last month and stepped up on that when edged out at Newmarket, drawn in the wrong place and finishing very well. He'll be in all the decent sprints this season and I think he'll win a Group 1 - he'll probably go straight to the Queen Elizabeth II Jubilee Stakes on the Saturday of Royal Ascot. race_betting_forecast: 4/1 Time For Sandals,  Big Mojo, 5/1 Kind Of Blue, 13/2 American Affair, 8/1 Cool Hoof Luke, 10/1 Aramram, 12/1 Quinault,  Regional, 14/1 Elmonjed, 16/1 Jasour, 20/1 Diligent Harry, 25/1 Crestofdistinction, 33/1 Ain't Nobody
runner_quotes: 2026-03-07 - "Cool Hoof Luke doesn't want to make the running but I didn't want to sit last because I thought we wouldn't go that fast. In an ideal situation he was going to be second or third with cover but I didn't achieve that. The horse did really well to save enough. He's a Gimcrack winner. The start before the Gimcrack we ran him at Goodwood because we felt he'd get seven furlongs and I made the running on him there and went too fast. I was worried about repeating the error but I got away with it. As long as the horse stays healthy, he has a very bright future. He's a beautiful model and that was quite a deep race, it was only his second run back in a year and a half, it was an excellent performance - Oisin Murphy, rider.", 2024-08-23 - "When you look at Cool Hoof Luke you think he's going to be a better 3yo don't you? We weren't sure about the trip, he's bred to be a sprinter, looks like a 7f horse and yet again the trainer got it right! We were a bit disappointed at Goodwood, but he came back and won today - Anna Lisa Balding representing Andrew Balding, trainer."

race_betting_forecast: 4/1 Time For Sandals,  Big Mojo, 5/1 Kind Of Blue, 13/2 American Affair, 8/1 Cool Hoof Luke, 10/1 Aramram, 12/1 Quinault,  Regional, 14/1 Elmonjed, 16/1 Jasour, 20/1 Diligent Harry, 25/1 Crestofdistinction, 33/1 Ain't Nobody
runner_quotes: 2025-05-05 - "Crestofdistinction has built well over the winter and he's always shown a lot of ability at home. He's had two racecourse gallops at Kempton prior to this and shaped up well from each one. We put the cheekpieces on because he's such a laidback horse and he's got the potential to keep improving into a nice sprinter. He's going the right way about things and I always thought he was quite smart. He was quite green last year but it was a much different story today, he pretty much dominated as soon as he jumped out - David Probert, jockey."
runner_stable_tour: "He was third and second on his two starts last year at Doncaster and Kempton and we decided to give him a little bit of a break. He had a setback at the start of last year, which knocked his summer out, but he's back in full training and doing well. He should be able to go and win his maiden and then go on to bigger and better things. He could really progress through the sprints and handicap division this year. 02-04-25"

race_betting_forecast: 4/1 Time For Sandals,  Big Mojo, 5/1 Kind Of Blue, 13/2 American Affair, 8/1 Cool Hoof Luke, 10/1 Aramram, 12/1 Quinault,  Regional, 14/1 Elmonjed, 16/1 Jasour, 20/1 Diligent Harry, 25/1 Crestofdistinction, 33/1 Ain't Nobody

I've ridden him out the last couple of weeks and Clive [Cox, trainer] was extremely positive on his chances. We've got him in the King Charles III at Royal Ascot as a stiff five furlongs is more in our favour - Clive Cox, trainer.", 2024-02-24 - "We were worried about the drop back to the minimum [trip], but Clive thought there would be plenty of pace on which would suit Diligent Harry and he was right. runner_stable_tour: "He ran well last week at Southwell when third under top weight, which was his best run of the year, and he now runs on All-Weather Championships finals day at Newcastle on Friday. He won at the meeting two years ago and I'm looking forward to running him. 17-04-25", "It was great to see him back to near his best when second in the Group 3 Hackwood Stakes at Newbury. He had enjoyed a good campaign before that, having won twice at Newcastle, the latter being the All-Weather Sprint Championships. The form of that race is pretty hot as the runner-up, Witch Hunter, went on to win at Royal Ascot, while further down the field Vadream has gone on to win the Group 3 Palace House Stakes at Newmarket. He has yet to win on grass, but his head second to Commanche Falls last time, one of the most consistent horses in training, shows that there are big races to be won with him. I think the Hopeful Stakes at Newmarket is a possible logical step after that fine effort. At some point we will give him a break before preparing him for the next all-weather season. 02-08-23"

race_betting_forecast: 4/1 Time For Sandals,  Big Mojo, 5/1 Kind Of Blue, 13/2 American Affair, 8/1 Cool Hoof Luke, 10/1 Aramram, 12/1 Quinault,  Regional, 14/1 Elmonjed, 16/1 Jasour, 20/1 Diligent Harry, 25/1 Crestofdistinction, 33/1 Ain't Nobody
runner_quotes: 2025-07-26 - "Jim [Crowley, jockey] said 'run him on Saturday, I would!' We'll see. It's a different sort of track but Elmonjed likes a big field and being covered up and helped through. He's not really happy being on his own in front as long as he was today but he hung on. He'd be better on faster ground too - Maureen Haggas, trainer's wife", 2024-08-24 - "The horse is tough since he's had the headgear put on him. Elmonjed was idling without it and needed it to make him concentrate. He hits flat spots in his races and it wouldn't amaze me if he got further one day. It's very much one step at a time with him, but he could be good enough for a bigger prize in time - Angus Gold, racing manager to Shadwell Estates.", 2023-09-07 - "It looked like the runner-up had got away from him a bit so Elmonjed did well to win under a penalty and I really liked how he put his head down in the final furlong. He surprised us a little at Lingfield and has really improved from his debut. He was gelded in the summer because he was misbehaving but he's a good-looking horse and progressing with time. He's entered at Chantilly next Saturday and that's a pretty quick turnaround, but we'll talk with Sheikha Hissa and William [Haggas] and make a plan. He's going in the right direction and is a really nice horse - Richard Hills, assistant racing manager for owners Shadwell.", 2023-08-21 - "Shadwell have another nice horse on their hands. Elmonjed has done well to overcome that greeness early on what is a sharp track - Tom Marquand, jockey speaking to Sky Sports Racing."

race_betting_forecast: 4/1 Time For Sandals,  Big Mojo, 5/1 Kind Of Blue, 13/2 American Affair, 8/1 Cool Hoof Luke, 10/1 Aramram, 12/1 Quinault,  Regional, 14/1 Elmonjed, 16/1 Jasour, 20/1 Diligent Harry, 25/1 Crestofdistinction, 33/1 Ain't Nobody
runner_quotes: 2024-06-21 - "That was a great run. I'm very pleased. Obviously the winner was impressive. Jasour just switched leads right before the line, which might have cost us second, but he's run a blinder. I'm thrilled he's going the right way. I think we are close enough to warrant a kick at the July Cup, a race in which three-year-olds have done really well in the past. That would be my first thoughts as they cross the line, and as long as he comes back ok from here, he's a horse who deserves to be in the top flight - Clive Cox, trainer.", 2024-05-01 - "Jasour was class last year in the July Stakes, then in the Morny they split and he was in the middle and just went too free. I think he got a little bit frightened as a juvenile, so we've worked and worked and he had some precious turnout up until Christmas at home. The whole team has worked really hard to be sure that we've kept him right and Jim [Crowley, jockey] seemed to really strike a chord with him in the July Stakes, so I'm just pleased the whole plan has come off. I was a little worried the ground would be too easy for him, but that didn't seem to be a problem. The Commonwealth Cup is absolutely the plan, and the Sandy Lane would be nice. We're looking forward to seeing him on a better surface and Jim has given him a super confidence-building ride. Winning was a super bonus I wasn't entirely sure would happen - Clive Cox, trainer.", 2023-07-13 - "It's the first time I'd ridden Jasour but riding him from off the pace has been the key to him - Jim Crowley, jockey."
runner_stable_tour: "He's won at the track and was third in last year's Commonwealth Cup. He was only sixth on his reappearance at Haydock but he didn't have a great lead-up with a bruised foot and he was a little bit fresh, so I'd hope he can take a big step forward from that. 10-06-25", "Right from day one he looked a little bit special. His homework suggested he was good enough to win on his debut, but he was a little unlucky there as he went down by a neck having jumped the road at Salisbury. He won next time out at Nottingham, again over 5f, but I thought he was crying out for an extra furlong on that occasion. He could have gone to Royal Ascot but he just wasn't ready. He progressed from his win to his third run in the July Stakes at Newmarket. I was positive about his chances there and he duly obliged, running out an impressive winner. We now have to decide which route to take. I am not one to waste my owner's money on lots of fancy entries, so you can tell how much I thought of him as I had given him an entry in the Gimcrack before he ran at Newmarket. But if he went to the Gimcrack he probably could not go for the Group 1 Prix Morny which is just over a week later, but if he goes to the Richmond at Goodwood on Thursday there would be the ideal time between races. So, at the moment I'm leaning towards Glorious Goodwood, but we will see how the horse is before finalising plans. 02-08-23"

race_betting_forecast: 4/1 Time For Sandals,  Big Mojo, 5/1 Kind Of Blue, 13/2 American Affair, 8/1 Cool Hoof Luke, 10/1 Aramram, 12/1 Quinault,  Regional, 14/1 Elmonjed, 16/1 Jasour, 20/1 Diligent Harry, 25/1 Crestofdistinction, 33/1 Ain't Nobody
runner_breeder: Mr & Mrs M Morris, Mr & Mrs P Hopper
runner_quotes: 2025-09-06 - "Kind Of Blue ran really well and I am just really pleased to see him run well again. He loves it at Ascot so we look forward to going back there again with him - James Fanshawe, trainer.", 2024-10-19 - "Kind Of Blue was unraced before the start of this season but has made giant strides and run some cracking races in defeat. He's improved with every race and there's no greater man than James Fanshawe to prepare a Champions Sprint winner here - he knows what he needs - James Doyle, rider.", 2024-09-07 - "It was a great run, I'm very pleased with Kind Of Blue. It looked like at the furlong pole we were going really well, but he's run a great race. He's come a long way and I think the bit easier ground helped him. He's getting the hang of it, he's a very exciting horse to have in the yard and I'd say there's a good chance we'll go to Ascot [on Champions Day] - James Fanshawe, trainer."

race_betting_forecast: 4/1 Time For Sandals,  Big Mojo, 5/1 Kind Of Blue, 13/2 American Affair, 8/1 Cool Hoof Luke, 10/1 Aramram, 12/1 Quinault,  Regional, 14/1 Elmonjed, 16/1 Jasour, 20/1 Diligent Harry, 25/1 Crestofdistinction, 33/1 Ain't Nobody
runner_quotes: 2026-02-14 - "Quinault is an absolute superstar. He's got an amazing will to win and if it's close, he just wants it - Stuart Williams, trainer.", 2025-06-28 - "Quinault is a fast horse and on his dam's side, he's definitely bred to go a bit further. Tom [Morley, joint-owner] continually have this argument whether he's best over a stiff six furlongs or an easy seven, but I think it's probably either. He's just a high-class horse who loves to win. Once he gets in front and rolling along at a place like this, he's hard to catch. We'd like to come back here to the Ebor meeting in August as it was a win and you're in race for it. We'll aim for that and be working back from there. He tries very hard and that's the key to it. It's his first Group win and it means a lot. I took him to Dubai in the winter and ran him in a Group 1, as we've always thought he was capable of this sort of thing - Stuart Williams, trainer.", 2024-09-08 - "We thought Quinault wouldn't like this ground but he just wants to win - he definitely tries. He's a special horse for a yard the size of ours, especially as he came a long way in such a short space of time last year. He had a little wobble earlier this year and was a little bit ill after we gave him the flu jab, but he's bang in form now. He's such a special horse. There's a chance he improves again next year and I still think he might be better over seven furlongs, so we've got to explore that avenue as well. He'll have a little bit of a break now as he's done his bit for the summer, but we could travel with him. He's a gelding so we'll go where the best prize-money is that he can win. There isn't much point in running him in Group races just for the sake of it - Stuart Williams, trainer.", 2024-08-24 - "I don't think Quinault was in love with the ground but he just tries very hard - Stuart Williams, trainer.", 2024-08-04 - "I'd love Quinault to go seven furlongs round a bend somewhere if I could find the right race for him - I think that would be ideal. We wouldn't be against travelling him later in the year. We might go abroad somewhere, some of the tighter tracks, maybe in America, somewhere like that might suit him - Stuart Williams, trainer.", 2023-09-09 - "What a special horse Quinault has been for everybody this year. He didn't run a bad race the last day but he's back on song today - it's a special day. He's got one way of running in that you let him stride on in front, and he's so genuine he keeps finding for you - Luke Catton, jockey.", 2023-07-13 - "We had Sendintank who won ten handicaps in a season, including four in a week twice, but that was at a much lower level than this - this is very different. Quinault is a very tough horse and he's got loads of talent as well. Luke Catton has done a fantastic job settling him and it's such a shame he cannot do the weight. The horse was just a runaway when he arrived, he would just bolt. We trained him on a treadmill for the first two months so he never left the yard, before we slowly started to introduce him to the Heath, so there's a lot of work gone into what you're seeing today. He's always had the talent, it's just been a case of letting the horse know it and letting him use it - he's always been fast, that wasn't the problem! He's bred to get further so as long as we can keep him settled in his races that's a route we'll look to go with him - Stuart Williams, trainer.", 2023-06-17 - "This was the biggest occasion Quinault has been to and he got a little bit warm in the preliminaries but he settled nicely in the race and Connor gave him a lovely ride, just managing to get him there on the line. We put him in at Ascot just in case something happened that forced our hand, but my gut feeling is probably not to run. I think he'll get a bit further and I've put him in the Bunbury Cup so that's a possibility - Stuart Williams, trainer.", 2023-05-20 - "It was unfortunate Luke Catton couldn't ride Quinault today as he couldn't do the weight. Luke has done a brilliant job settling him at home as he was a tearaway when he came but that is why Godolphin put him in the sale in the first place. He will probably go to York now for the valuable three-year-old six-furlong handicap as long as he comes out of this fine - Stuart Williams, trainer."

race_betting_forecast: 4/1 Time For Sandals,  Big Mojo, 5/1 Kind Of Blue, 13/2 American Affair, 8/1 Cool Hoof Luke, 10/1 Aramram, 12/1 Quinault,  Regional, 14/1 Elmonjed, 16/1 Jasour, 20/1 Diligent Harry, 25/1 Crestofdistinction, 33/1 Ain't Nobody
runner_breeder: Razza Del Sole Societa Agricola Srl (Gb)
runner_quotes: 2024-06-18 - "Another half-a-furlong and we might have got there but that's horseracing. We can be nothing but proud of Regional. I would imagine we will try and aim towards something like the July Cup now - Ed Bethell, trainer.", 2023-09-09 - "It was a massive performance. It looked smooth and Regional was one of the last to come off the bridle. In the Nunthorpe, he was rushed off his feet but he was so much more comfortable at this trip and I'm over the moon for everyone. The owners might want to go to America but I'll see how the horse is first and then we'll make a decision - Ed Bethell, trainer.", 2023-06-10 - "Fast ground and flat tracks are his big things and we'll try and keep Regional to that now. I'd imagine we'll look at the Nunthorpe in August as it looks a logical target. We'll chat about what we do in the interim - there's the City Walls with a penalty or the King George at Goodwood. He wasn't the same horse last season as he is this year, he's just thrived and is enjoying his races - Ed Bethell, trainer.", 2023-05-18 - "I wasn't expecting to make the running, but Regional is a decent horse and always has been. He came third in a Stewards' Cup and was unlucky in other handicaps, so I'm delighted for him. He ran off 100 in the Stewards' Cup and I think that's his mark in handicaps, which might make life difficult, but there's the City Walls back here now he's shown he's electric. I didn't think he was and he's shown me he's quick enough - Ed Bethell, trainer.", 2021-09-03 - "We thought we'd have to pay 30,000 and we paid 3,500gns and I'm not sure why. Regional's work had been good at home before and we were hopeful but I was a little unsure whether he'd strip fit enough for today. He's got a nice future, that's for sure, I'm looking forward to him - Ed Bethell, trainer.", 2020-07-23 - "Regional travelled well and was very professional - Tony Hamilton, jockey."
runner_stable_tour: "He's exciting. I was delighted with him in Ireland, the stiff 6f at the Curragh probably isn't his bag, so he'll go for the King Charles and I hope he'll run well over the stiff 5f at Ascot. He's come out of the race well and he's got a chance, but it's only a chance. It's going to be a tough gig. 10-06-24", "He's a legend and must be one of the most consistent sprinters around. He never runs a bad race in a Group 1 - he hasn't been out of the first three at the top level apart from in the Nunthorpe once. He ran well at Ascot, but the older he's getting the more distance he probably wants so we'll take him up to six furlongs and look at the Prix Maurice de Gheest at Deauville and then go back to the Sprint Cup at Haydock. He's as good as ever at seven. He hasn't been overraced and the owners are reaping the rewards. He's taken us all on a journey none of us could ever believe he would. We're very lucky to have him. 02-07-25"

race_betting_forecast: 4/1 Time For Sandals,  Big Mojo, 5/1 Kind Of Blue, 13/2 American Affair, 8/1 Cool Hoof Luke, 10/1 Aramram, 12/1 Quinault,  Regional, 14/1 Elmonjed, 16/1 Jasour, 20/1 Diligent Harry, 25/1 Crestofdistinction, 33/1 Ain't Nobody
runner_owner: D Bevan And Mrs D Bevan
runner_quotes: 2025-06-20 - "It was very smooth in that we'd planned to follow the American horse [Shisospicy]. Prior to today the draw was a concern but with the first race winner coming from a low draw it gave us the confidence to stay with our plan; we didn't have much choice anyway. Time For Sandals had just been training very well and a good strong pace to aim at was what she needed and Richard [Kingscote] gave her a fantastic ride - Harry Eustace, trainer.", 2024-08-22 - "I'm delighted, it was a bit of a leap into the unknown. Time For Sandals travelled very sweetly and lack of experience probably cost her. We may have to look at black type races - Harry Eustace, trainer."

race_betting_forecast: 5/4 Legacy Link, 5/2 Felicitas, 4/1 Moments Of Joy, 6/1 Sea The Storm, 20/1 K Sarra, 66/1 Della Pace
runner_stable_tour: "She's going to be stepped up to ten furlongs. She's done well over the winter and we're looking forward to getting her started. 02-04-26"

race_betting_forecast: 5/4 Legacy Link, 5/2 Felicitas, 4/1 Moments Of Joy, 6/1 Sea The Storm, 20/1 K Sarra, 66/1 Della Pace
runner_quotes: 2026-04-24 - "It's hard to know when you come into these races but I think there were a few reputations on the fence. We thought a lot about Felicitas, we were debating whether to go into a trial or coming here for more experience. We were a little intimidated but we stuck to our guns and I think she'll be heading to a trial now, probably York [Musidora] - Ed Walker, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.", 2025-12-15 - "We're thrilled. Felicitas will be better for going further next year, but we're very impressed. We expected her to run well, but perhaps not quite that emphatically on debut. She's an exciting filly, and we'll see where we start her in the spring. We'll see how she's working, as she's a big filly with lots of scope and is very exciting for next year - Ed Walker, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "She's very exciting and will have an Oaks campaign, whether it be Epsom or Chantilly - I'm not convinced she'll stay 1m4f. She's done well physically and could start in a fillies' novice at Sandown or the Pretty Polly Stakes at Newmarket. We've always thought a lot of her. We were pleased with her winning debut at Lingfield and we've been happy with her this spring. 22-04-26"

race_betting_forecast: 5/4 Legacy Link, 5/2 Felicitas, 4/1 Moments Of Joy, 6/1 Sea The Storm, 20/1 K Sarra, 66/1 Della Pace
runner_quotes: 2025-11-26 - "K Sarra still has a lot to learn, she was very green still. She's done everything right so far, her works been okay at home and she's bound to improve quite a bit. She's bred to be a decent filly so we will see - Rossa Ryan, jockey."
runner_stable_tour: "She ran in the Fred Darling and has come out of the race well. She'd had only one start on the all-weather and she had been working well. She'll step up to a mile and a quarter next as we thought she might be better at middle distances. She's a sister to Pride Of Arras and they're a family who catch on quick, and she's pretty straightforward. 27-04-26"

race_betting_forecast: 5/4 Legacy Link, 5/2 Felicitas, 4/1 Moments Of Joy, 6/1 Sea The Storm, 20/1 K Sarra, 66/1 Della Pace
runner_quotes: 2025-09-05 - "Legacy Link is a very well-bred filly and feels very progressive. There's still plenty of improvement to come but she could end up there [Fillies' Mile] - Colin Keane, jockey."

I was a bit worried the stiff six [furlongs] wouldn't suit but, having said that, he saw it out well. He's a work in progress and he'll improve a lot for that. The Mill Reef [at Newbury in October] could be a nice race for him  - Karl Burke, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.The penny didn't really drop until I gave Gold Queen Kindly a couple of flicks to persuade him to go forward but he knuckled down well and hit the line strong. race_betting_forecast: 6/1 Minhad, 13/2 Mafting, 7/1 Great Bedwyn, 8/1 Lightening Company, 9/1 Master Builder, 10/1 Galyx,  Fox Journey, 11/1 Jez Bomb, 12/1 Hermetic, 14/1 Military Cross,  Arrange,  Dwindling Funds, 16/1 Helmsley, 20/1 Sportingsilvermine,  Project Geofin, 25/1 Free World, 33/1 Marhaba The Champ
runner_quotes: 2024-05-06 - "I was very impressed with how professional Galyx was. He's taken a long time mentally to mature and I did think today might catch him out again, especially with the tight turns, but he showed a bit of class today and he's a nice horse. We won't overface him too soon. Something like a nice handicap at Glorious Goodwood would be on the cards, after another novice in the next month or so, but I'll be excited about him as a 4yo - Jack Channon, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He's a lovely four-year-old who's very lightly raced. He's had only five races, but he won on his last start at Leicester in October over 1m2f and is now rated 87. He'll be a nice horse to have a crack at those nice 1m2f handicaps. He's the type to go for something like the John Smith's Cup and he should improve as the year goes on. 02-04-25"

race_race_name: Stuey Weston & Friends Getting Out Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_betting_forecast: 6/1 Minhad, 13/2 Mafting, 7/1 Great Bedwyn, 8/1 Lightening Company, 9/1 Master Builder, 10/1 Galyx,  Fox Journey, 11/1 Jez Bomb, 12/1 Hermetic, 14/1 Military Cross,  Arrange,  Dwindling Funds, 16/1 Helmsley, 20/1 Sportingsilvermine,  Project Geofin, 25/1 Free World, 33/1 Marhaba The Champ
runner_quotes: 2024-09-07 - "Master Builder did something really nice in the Melrose and we were quite confident that with a bit of cut in the ground, he would improve again. He switches off easily and quickly and they went very fast early on, so I wasn't too worried. You could see he was picking up the bridle and travelling better than anything else - David Menuisier, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "His form from Goodwood in May has worked out well with the winner, French Master, going on to win at Royal Ascot. He'll run in the 1m6f handicap at Goodwood on Saturday and, following on from what he does there, we'll see about the Ebor. He has an entry for it but it looks a fancy one at this stage as he's only rated 94. We'd consider it if he goes up in the weights. 30-07-25"

race_race_name: Stuey Weston & Friends Getting Out Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_betting_forecast: 6/1 Minhad, 13/2 Mafting, 7/1 Great Bedwyn, 8/1 Lightening Company, 9/1 Master Builder, 10/1 Galyx,  Fox Journey, 11/1 Jez Bomb, 12/1 Hermetic, 14/1 Military Cross,  Arrange,  Dwindling Funds, 16/1 Helmsley, 20/1 Sportingsilvermine,  Project Geofin, 25/1 Free World, 33/1 Marhaba The Champ
runner_quotes: 2025-05-17 - "Minhad was a bit free at Haydock last time but David Egan got him settled this time and he finished off well. We were worried about the ground as ideally he wants it soft. He's in the Italian Derby but that will come too soon for him - Jose Santos, assistant to winning trainer Ismael Mohammed."

runner_quotes: 2023-08-25 - "Ryan [Moore] said he just jumped a little bit awkwardly and that they didn't go as quickly as they might have. Marhaba The Champ was running a little bit free with him but he relaxed and let him fill up. He's done it well and that was a class ride. He'll probably get further but needs to relax a bit more. That's an avenue for the future but we'll probably stick to this trip for now - Kevin Ryan, trainer.", 2023-05-19 - "We thought Marhaba The Champ would run a big race and William (Buick) gave him a fantastic ride. He's a horse we like a lot, we've just been waiting for the ground. He loves quick ground without any juice in it at all - it's so important to him. I could see him going a bit further in time - Kevin Ryan, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He's a cracking horse and looks to have done well again over the winter. He loves York and fast ground so we'll aim him at the Dante meeting, but we could explore going up in trip at some stage this season. 04-04-24"

He's done nothing wrong up to now and there's a chance he might handle a little bit more ease in the ground - David O'Meara, trainer, speaking to Racing TV."

runner_stable_tour: "He's a little star who's done really well this year, winning four times and finishing second in the straight mile series final at Redcar. He wants a sixth furlong and I don't know where we'll go, but there might be a race at Pontefract coming up, which could suit him. He's very straightforward and we like him. runner_quotes: 2023-06-17 - "Ideally a really hard mile is what Connemara Coast wants, when he's almost off the bridle behind, and then he'll outstay them, but he'll definitely be a mile and quarter horse next season. He might go to the July meeting but Ryan thought there'd be an Ascot handicap for him at the end of the year. When they go flat out at Ascot it will be perfect for him - Richard Hughes, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He has never run a bad race in his six starts to date, which includes a win at Wolverhampton last autumn and at Sandown on his most recent outing. Ryan [Moore] won on him at the Esher track and it is always great to get him, but he is a man in serious demand and it is not that easy to get him on one of yours. He is now on a 3lb higher mark of 86 and the plan is to head back to either Newmarket for the July meeting next month where there is a three-year-old 0-95 mile handicap, or Ascot a day later for a 0-95 for three-year-olds. We will try to get Ryan again as he remains on a very workable mark. 29-06-23"

runner_quotes: 2025-07-11 - "It was a nice performance. Henry and all the lads at home liked her and we were hopeful of a good run today. Salsinha is  French bred and has loads of pace. She quickened up nicely on that ground - John Gleeson, jockey."
runner_stable_tour: "She's a nice filly and looked very impressive when winning her bumper over the summer at Cork. We're looking forward to going hurdling with her in the next few weeks. 11-11-25"

runner_quotes: 2025-04-06 - "Switch From Diesel is a classy filly and she seems to have improved from run to run as the season's gone on. She's very exciting and she's probably as good a horse as I've had for a while. If everything stayed right with her she could be very good next season. The plan is to go to Punchestown and then put her out on summer's grass and go hurdling next year. She did an extra year in bumpers and Paul [Mullins, owner] was keen to do that. That was his plan and he stuck to it. She's turned into a real happy filly and that was very impressive. She has a high cruising speed and she'll make a lovely novice mare next year - Ross O'Sullivan, trainer."

runner_quotes: 2025-06-15 - "It was a nice bit of improvement from the last day. Based on his form Sneddy Eddie needed soft ground and got it today so we fancied him. He had slipped in the weights and we were claiming 7lb off him. Patrick McGettigan is a very nice jockey. He's a good rider and does what he's told and I will keep using him in the future. He's a horse that needs cut in the ground so we'll pick and choose where we go with him. We left home this morning at 8am and we fed him at 6.30am. He deserved to win a race for us - Noel Kelly, trainer."

runner_quotes: 2026-02-19 - "Rideau Canal had promised a lot, but that was a very long time coming. He was a bit non-committal about everything, so I jumped him over banks and ditches hoping to ignite his enthusiasm, which I think it did. I wasn't expecting this but he was very good, and I'm delighted for his owner Jules Sigler who also won with Westandtogether at Navan recently. Georgie gave him a gorgeous ride and when I was doing my Gordon Elliott gig, she won the Ladies Derby in the Curragh, so she has sort-of ridden for me! She is well able to ride and did exactly what was needed to be done today - Denise Foster, trainer."
